EigenInference.claim: Main SwiftUI App struct with NSApplicationDelegateAdaptor for activation policy management
Central ObservableObject managing provider state with @Published properties
ProviderManager as ObservableObject managing darkbloom subprocess lifecycle
CLIRunner class centralizes darkbloom binary execution with path resolution
ConfigManager enum provides TOML parsing for shared provider.toml configuration
Swift package with zero external dependencies, only built-in Apple frameworks
App starts as .accessory (menu bar only) and switches to .regular when windows open
Real-time provider status polling via HTTP health checks on port 8100
Auto-restart logic with exponential backoff and maximum retry limits
Hardware detection using sysctlbyname and system_profiler commands
Shared configuration path with CLI tools via ~/.darkbloom/provider.toml
Telemetry reporter for crash analytics and error reporting to coordinator backend
HuggingFace cache directory scanning for MLX model discovery
Keychain Services integration for secure API key storage
MenuBarView as primary user interface with ObservedObject pattern
Comprehensive design system with adaptive color palettes for light/dark modes
Modern Swift Testing framework usage with @Test syntax instead of XCTest
Multiple SwiftUI scenes including MenuBarExtra, Settings, and various windows
Environment setup matching CLIRunner for consistent tool discovery
Earnings and wallet integration via coordinator HTTP endpoints

EigenInferenceEnclave.claim: SecureEnclaveIdentity manages hardware-bound P-256 signing keys
Creates new identity by generating P-256 key in Secure Enclave
AttestationBlob contains hardware identity and security state fields
AttestationService creates attestations with deterministic JSON encoding
FFI bridge provides C-callable functions with proper memory management
FFI sign function takes raw data and returns base64-encoded signature
CLI attest command generates ephemeral attestations with optional parameters
getChipName() uses system_profiler to extract Apple Silicon chip information
checkSIPEnabled() calls csrutil status to determine SIP state
JSON encoder uses sortedKeys for deterministic output matching Go implementation
Sign operation returns DER-encoded ECDSA signature compatible with Go crypto/ecdsa
Public key is exported as raw 64-byte representation (X||Y without prefix)
FFI provides Secure Enclave availability check function
Full attestation function supports optional encryption key and binary hash binding
Static verify method re-encodes attestation for signature validation
Package provides both static library and executable CLI tool products
Identity creation produces 64-byte raw P-256 public keys
Attestation service creates verifiable signed attestation blobs
JSON output uses sorted keys for deterministic encoding
RDMA check ensures remote memory access is disabled for security

EigenInferenceEnclaveCLI.claim: CLI imports CryptoKit and EigenInferenceEnclave, provides command-line attestation tool
printUsage function defines command structure with attest and info commands
cmdAttest function handles attestation generation with optional parameters
cmdInfo function displays Secure Enclave availability and ephemeral key info
Secure Enclave availability is checked before operations
Creates ephemeral SecureEnclaveIdentity and AttestationService for each invocation
Uses JSONEncoder with sortedKeys for deterministic output
Main function provides argument parsing and command dispatch
Manual argument parsing handles --encryption-key and --binary-hash options
WebSocketBridge is a stub indicating removed TLS bridge functionality
CLI is built as executable target named eigeninference-enclave
CLI target depends on EigenInferenceEnclave library
Requires macOS 13.0 or later for Secure Enclave support

analytics.claim: The service entry point initializes structured logging and loads configuration with error handling
Backend selection pattern allows memory or PostgreSQL storage based on configuration
Configuration structure includes address, backend type, database URL, CORS origin, and pseudonym secret
Backend-specific validation ensures required parameters for PostgreSQL mode
Health check endpoint tests backend connectivity and returns status with timestamp
Earnings leaderboard endpoint supports scope, window, and limit query parameters with validation
Leaderboard supports account and node scopes for different aggregation levels
Time window filtering supports 24h, 7d, 30d, and all-time periods
Service layer handles query normalization, validation, and pseudonym generation for leaderboard entries
PostgreSQL store implementation uses connection pooling and validates connectivity on initialization
PostgreSQL overview query uses CTEs for efficient provider and earnings statistics aggregation
Pseudonym generator uses HMAC-SHA256 with kind prefixing to create deterministic human-readable aliases
Alias format uses predefined adjectives, animals, and numbers for human-readable pseudonyms
Primary external dependency is pgx/v5 for PostgreSQL connectivity
Service is designed to sit beside coordinator as standalone read-only analytics service
PostgreSQL mode requires read-only user with SELECT privileges on analytics tables

coordinator.claim: The coordinator runs in a GCP Confidential VM with hardware-encrypted memory and acts as central routing layer
Server struct implements hexagonal architecture with clear separation between external interfaces and business logic
Registry manages 1000+ concurrent providers with trust levels and intelligent routing
Store interface abstracts persistence layer supporting PostgreSQL and in-memory backends
Payment system uses double-entry accounting with micro-USD precision for blockchain compatibility
Primary runtime dependencies include WebSocket, PostgreSQL, JWT, and cryptographic libraries
API routes include OpenAI-compatible inference endpoints and administrative interfaces
Scoring algorithm considers provider load, hardware specs, trust level, and model cache state
Request timeouts and retry logic handle provider failures and capacity constraints
Supports both PostgreSQL for production and in-memory store for development with explicit safety checks
Provider registration includes model catalog filtering and hardware attestation validation
Authentication supports both Privy JWTs and API keys with different access levels
Rate limiting uses per-account token buckets with separate tiers for inference vs financial operations
Datadog integration provides APM tracing, metrics, and log forwarding
MDM integration enables independent provider security verification via MicroMDM
End-to-end encryption uses X25519 keys derived from BIP39 mnemonic for coordinator identity
Background eviction loop removes stale providers every 30 seconds based on heartbeat timeout
Model catalog seeding includes hardcoded models with size and architecture metadata
Deployment includes MicroMDM and step-ca integration with environment configuration
Middleware stack includes CORS, panic recovery, and structured request logging

darkbloom.claim: Darkbloom is the provider agent for Apple Silicon Macs with comprehensive architecture covering hardware detection, model management, backend lifecycle, coordinator connection, private text engine, attestation, and E2E crypto
CatalogModel struct defines the model metadata structure with fields for ID, display name, size, architecture, description, and memory requirements
The component is named 'darkbloom' version 0.4.7 with description 'EigenInference provider agent for Apple Silicon Macs' and builds a binary at src/main.rs
WebSocket client manages provider connection with automatic reconnection, registration, heartbeats, inference requests, attestation challenges, and result forwarding
Security module implements runtime protections including PT_DENY_ATTACH, SIP verification, and memory wiping to prevent inspection of inference data
Backend management supports vllm-mlx and mlx-lm with health monitoring, automatic restart, and BackendManager wrapper for lifecycle management
Hardware detection queries macOS system APIs for memory size, CPU cores, machine model, and GPU capabilities using sysctl and system_profiler
NodeKeyPair implements ephemeral X25519 key pairs for E2E encryption, generated fresh on each launch and protected by PT_DENY_ATTACH + Hardened Runtime + SIP
Model scanning examines HuggingFace cache for MLX models, filtering by available memory with detection heuristics for directory names containing 'mlx' and quantization hints
Hypervisor integration uses Apple's framework to create lightweight VM with Stage 2 page tables for memory isolation, requiring 16MB alignment and pre-allocated pools
Request proxy handles legacy/local flows between coordinator WebSocket and local backends, with E2E decryption happening before proxy handling
Service management provides launchd user agent integration with explicit user control - only runs when started via 'darkbloom start' or app toggle, no auto-start on login
Telemetry architecture uses multiple sources (tracing layer, panic hook, backend scraper, direct emit) feeding into mpsc queue, async batcher, and HTTPS POST to coordinator with disk overflow

Dependencies include tokio for async runtime, reqwest for HTTP, tokio-tungstenite for WebSocket, axum for HTTP server, serde for serialization, crypto_box for NaCl encryption, and security-framework for macOS integration
CLI interface provides comprehensive subcommands including init, serve, install, models, earnings, doctor, start/stop, logs, update, login/logout, and autoupdate configuration
Build-time coordinator URLs default to production (api.darkbloom.dev) with R2 CDN URLs for model and site-packages distribution, configurable via environment variables
Build system integrates Swift Secure Enclave library, linking EigenInferenceEnclave static library with Foundation, Security, and CryptoKit frameworks plus Swift runtime paths

Wire protocol defines JSON-encoded WebSocket messages with provider-to-coordinator messages (Register, Heartbeat, InferenceResponse, AttestationResponse) and coordinator-to-provider messages (InferenceRequest, Cancel, AttestationChallenge)
Service startup includes single-instance enforcement via PID file, process cleanup, hypervisor VM creation, security posture verification, and core dump disable before processing any inference data
Coordinator connection is deliberately delayed until all backends are loaded to prevent routing requests before the provider can serve them, ensuring service readiness

decrypt-test.claim: Component serves as a cross-language compatibility testing tool for NaCl Box decryption
Main entry point validates exactly 3 CLI arguments with usage message
Input validation ensures 32-byte keys and minimum 24-byte ciphertext per NaCl specifications
Key material is converted from byte arrays to crypto_box types for NaCl operations
Decryption extracts nonce from ciphertext prefix and performs AEAD decryption
Output handler validates UTF-8 and writes plaintext to stdout
Component depends on crypto_box 0.9 for NaCl operations and base64 0.22 for encoding
Uses base64 STANDARD engine and crypto_box AEAD traits for cryptographic operations
Go tests verify cross-language encryption compatibility by building and invoking this Rust binary
Test suite builds release binary and executes it with encrypted payloads
Cross-language test encrypts with Go e2e package and decrypts with this Rust utility
Test scenarios include JSON, empty, Unicode, large, and multiple round-trip payloads
Go encryption uses NaCl Box with nonce prefix format compatible with this Rust decryptor
All inputs are base64 decoded with error handling and process exit on failure

verify-attestation.claim: Simple package structure importing only fmt, os, and internal attestation library
File reading from fixed path /tmp/eigeninference_attestation.json with error handling
JSON parsing and verification delegation to attestation.VerifyJSON
Hardware identity extraction and display from verification result
Verification status display and exit code management
Cross-language verification success message indicating Swift to Go validation
Fixed file path for attestation input at /tmp/eigeninference_attestation.json
Exit code 1 for file read errors
Attestation package provides cross-language verification for Secure Enclave signed blobs
VerifyJSON function used by verify-attestation for attestation verification
Verification checks P-256 ECDSA signatures and security requirements
Go module definition with Go 1.25.0 requirement

web.claim: External dependencies analysis including Next.js, React, Privy, and cryptographic libraries
Layered architecture with provider wrapping and component structure
Main chat interface component with state management integration
Streaming chat function with optional encryption support
Privy-based authentication with automatic API key provisioning
Zustand state management with persistence for chat history
NaCl Box encryption implementation for coordinator communication
Client-side X.509 certificate verification against Apple Enterprise Attestation Root CA
App shell layout wrapper with conditional rendering based on authentication
Next.js API route for chat proxy with streaming and encryption support
Trust metadata header passthrough for hardware attestation verification
Sealed response handling for encrypted streaming events
Chat message handling with system prompt and trust tracking
API key provisioning flow using Privy access tokens
Privy authentication provider configuration with email login
TweetNaCl library usage for X25519 and Box encryption
Datadog Real User Monitoring integration for performance tracking
Google Analytics 4 integration with privacy controls and consent management
Environment variables configuration for coordinator URL and Privy app ID
Provider dashboard for hardware attestation monitoring and earnings tracking
Next.js configuration with TypeScript build optimization
